chunks.text: Nimbus Cloud Services - Customer Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) Q1: What is Nimbus Cloud Services? A: Nimbus is a cloud hosting provider offering virtual servers, managed databases, and object storage. We serve startups and mid-sized businesses across 12 regions worldwide. Q2: How is pricing calculated? A: Pricing is pay-as-you-go, billed per hour of compute usage. Storage is billed per GB per month. There are no upfront fees and no long-term contracts. A detailed breakdown is available on the billing dashboard. Q3: Do you offer a free tier? A: Yes. New customers get a free tier that includes 1 virtual server (1 vCPU, 1 GB RAM) and 10 GB of object storage for the first 12 months. Q4: What is your uptime guarantee? A: Our Service Level Agreement (SLA) guarantees 99.95% monthly uptime. If

12 months. Q4: What is your uptime guarantee? A: Our Service Level Agreement (SLA) guarantees 99.95% monthly uptime. If uptime falls below this, customers receive service credits proportional to the downtime experienced. Q5: How do I get technical support? A: Free and Standard plans include email support with a 24-hour response time. Premium plans include 24/7 live chat and phone support with a 1-hour response time. Q6: Can I upgrade or downgrade my plan? A: Yes, plan changes can be made anytime from the dashboard. Upgrades take effect immediately. Downgrades take effect at the start of the next billing cycle. Q7: How is my data backed up? A: Managed databases are automatically backed up every 6 hours, with backups retained for 30 days. Customers can also trigger manual snapshots at any ti

ically backed up every 6 hours, with backups retained for 30 days. Customers can also trigger manual snapshots at any time. Q8: Which payment methods do you accept? A: We accept major credit cards, PayPal, and bank transfers. Bank transfers are available only for annual Premium plans. Q9: How do I cancel my account? A: You can cancel anytime from the account settings page. Billing stops at the end of the current cycle. Stored data is permanently deleted 30 days after cancellation. Q10: Where are your data centers located? A: We operate in 12 regions including North America, Europe, India, Singapore, and Australia. You can choose your preferred region when creating a resource.

Nimbus Cloud Services - Internal Engineering Notes Project: "Stratus" Migration | Author: Platform Team OVERVIEW We are migrating our core billing service from a monolith to microservices. The target completion date is Q3 2026. The goal is to reduce deployment time and isolate failures so one service outage does not take down billing. ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS - API Gateway: We chose Kong over NGINX because Kong's plugin ecosystem handles rate-limiting and auth without custom code. - Database: Each microservice owns its own PostgreSQL instance. We avoid shared databases to prevent tight coupling between services. - Messaging: We use Apache Kafka for async events (e.g., "invoice.created"). This decouples producers from consumers and allows replaying events. CURRENT BLOCKERS 1. The legacy billi

ice.created"). This decouples producers from consumers and allows replaying events. CURRENT BLOCKERS 1. The legacy billing module uses stored procedures that are hard to replicate in the new service. Estimated 3 weeks to rewrite the logic in application code. 2. Latency spikes observed when the payment service calls the external Stripe API during peak hours. We are adding a retry queue as a fix. 3. The search feature using OpenSearch returns stale results because the indexing job runs only once per hour. We plan to move to near-real-time indexing via Kafka consumers. DEPLOYMENT - We deploy using Kubernetes with rolling updates. Each service has a minimum of 2 replicas for high availability. - CI/CD runs through GitHub Actions. A merge to "main" triggers automated tests, then a staged rollo

high availability. - CI/CD runs through GitHub Actions. A merge to "main" triggers automated tests, then a staged rollout to 10% of traffic before full release. MONITORING - Metrics: Prometheus + Grafana dashboards. - Logs: Centralized in the ELK stack. - Alerts: PagerDuty notifies the on-call engineer if error rate exceeds 2%. NEXT STEPS - Finish rewriting the billing stored procedures by mid-July. - Run a load test simulating 10,000 concurrent users before go-live. - Document the rollback procedure in case the migration fails.

Nimbus Cloud Services - Company Policy Document Last Updated: March 2026 1. WORKING HOURS Standard working hours are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Monday to Friday. Employees are expected to complete 40 hours per week. Core collaboration hours, during which all employees must be available, are 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM. 2. REMOTE WORK All employees are eligible for hybrid work. A minimum of 2 days per week must be spent in the office. Fully remote arrangements require manager approval and are reviewed every 6 months. 3. LEAVE POLICY - Annual Leave: 24 paid days per calendar year. - Sick Leave: 12 paid days per year. A medical certificate is required for absences longer than 3 consecutive days. - Parental Leave: 26 weeks for primary caregivers, 8 weeks for secondary caregivers. - Unused annual leave: A max

days. - Parental Leave: 26 weeks for primary caregivers, 8 weeks for secondary caregivers. - Unused annual leave: A maximum of 10 days may be carried over to the next year. Any excess is forfeited on December 31. 4. EXPENSE REIMBURSEMENT Work-related expenses must be submitted within 30 days of purchase through the internal Expensify portal. Expenses above 500 USD require pre-approval from a department head. Reimbursements are processed within 14 business days. 5. DATA SECURITY All employees must use the company VPN when accessing internal systems remotely. Passwords must be rotated every 90 days. Sharing customer data over personal email or messaging apps is strictly prohibited and may result in termination. 6. EQUIPMENT Each employee is issued a company laptop. Hardware refresh occurs e

ohibited and may result in termination. 6. EQUIPMENT Each employee is issued a company laptop. Hardware refresh occurs every 3 years. Lost or damaged equipment must be reported to IT within 24 hours. 7. CODE OF CONDUCT Nimbus maintains a zero-tolerance policy toward harassment and discrimination. Violations should be reported to HR at hr@nimbuscloud.com. All reports are treated confidentially.
